features.summary: NVIDIA reported record Q2 2025 revenue of $30 billion, up 15% sequentially and 122% year-over-year, driven primarily by data center revenue of $26.3 billion (up 154% YoY). The company executed a mask change on Blackwell GPUs to improve yields and expects to ship several billion dollars in Blackwell revenue in Q4. Hopper demand remains strong with continued growth expected in H2. Gaming revenue grew 16% YoY to $2.88 billion. The company guided Q3 revenue to $32.5 billion and expects full-year gross margins in the mid-70% range.
features.management_outlook: Management expects significant growth in fiscal 2026 driven by Blackwell ramp and continued Hopper strength. They see accelerating generative AI momentum across frontier models, enterprise AI adoption, and sovereign AI investments reaching low-double-digit billions this year. The company anticipates software revenue approaching a $2 billion annual run rate by year-end. Management views the current period as the beginning of modernizing $1 trillion worth of global data centers from general-purpose to accelerated computing infrastructure.
features.risks: The primary risk is the Blackwell production ramp execution following the mask change, though management indicates no functional changes were required. Gross margins face pressure from new product mix transitions and inventory provisions for low-yielding Blackwell materials. China market remains competitive with revenue below pre-export control levels. Supply constraints continue with Blackwell demand significantly exceeding supply expectations into next year.
features.key_drivers: Data center growth is driven by strong demand for AI training and inference workloads, with inference representing over 40% of data center revenue. Cloud service providers represent 45% of data center revenue while enterprise and consumer internet companies drive over 50%. Networking revenue doubled sequentially with Spectrum-X Ethernet platform gaining hundreds of customers. Enterprise AI adoption is accelerating with Fortune 100 companies implementing AI initiatives across multiple industries and use cases.
